search_text: Contrastive Retrospection: honing in on critical steps for rapid learning and generalization in RL 对比回顾：磨练强化学习快速学习和泛化的关键步骤 In real life, success is often contingent upon multiple critical steps that are distant in time from each other and from the final reward. These critical steps are challenging to identify with traditional reinforcement learning (RL) methods that rely on the Bellman equation for credit assignment. Here, we present a new RL algorithm that uses offline contrastive learning to hone in on these critical steps. This algorithm, which we call Contrastive Retrospection (ConSpec), can be added to any existing RL algorithm. ConSpec learns a set of prototypes for the critical steps in a task by a novel contrastive loss and delivers an intrinsic reward when the current state matches one of the prototypes. The prototypes in ConSpec provide two key benefits for credit assignment: (i) They enable rapid identification of all the critical steps. (ii) They do so in a readily interpretable manner, enabling out-of-distribution generalization when sensory features are altered. Distinct from other contemporary RL approaches to credit assignment, ConSpec takes advantage of the fact that it is easier to retrospectively identify the small set of steps that success is contingent upon (and ignoring other states) than it is to prospectively predict reward at every taken step. ConSpec greatly improves learning in a diverse set of RL tasks. The code is available at the link: https://github.com/sunchipsster1/ConSpec 在现实生活中，成功往往取决于多个关键步骤，这些步骤在时间上和最终奖励上相距甚远。这些关键步骤对于依赖贝尔曼方程进行学分分配的传统强化学习 (RL) 方法来说是具有挑战性的。在这里，我们提出了一种新的强化学习算法，该算法使用离线对比学习来磨练这些关键步骤。该算法称为对比回顾 (ConSpec)，可以添加到任何现有的 RL 算法中。 ConSpec 通过新颖的对比损失来学习任务中关键步骤的一组原型，并在当前状态与其中一个原型匹配时提供内在奖励。 ConSpec 中的原型为学分分配提供了两个主要优势：(i) 它们能够快速识别所有关键步骤。 (ii) 它们以易于解释的方式做到这一点，当感官特征改变时能够实现分布外泛化。与其他当代 RL 信用分配方法不同，ConSpec 利用了这样一个事实：回顾性地识别成功所依赖的一小部分步骤（并忽略其他状态）比前瞻性地预测每个步骤的奖励更容易。 ConSpec 极大地改善了各种 RL 任务的学习。代码可从以下链接获取：https://github.com/sunchipsster1/ConSpec 本文提出了一种名为ConSpec的强化学习算法，通过离线对比学习识别任务中的关键步骤，以提升学习效率和泛化能力。 Chen Sun,Wannan Yang,Thomas Jiralerspong,Dane Malenfant,Benjamin Alsbury-Nealy,Yoshua Bengio,Blake Richards cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Bowen Zhang,Zhi Tian,Quan Tang,Xiangxiang Chu,Xiaolin Wei,Chunhua Shen,Yifan Liu
title: SegViT: Semantic Segmentation with Plain Vision Transformers
title_zh: SegViT：使用普通视觉 Transformer 进行语义分割
abstract: We explore the capability of plain Vision Transformers (ViTs) for semantic segmentation and propose the SegVit. Previous ViT-based segmentation networks usually learn a pixel-level representation from the output of the ViT. Differently, we make use of the fundamental component -- attention mechanism, to generate masks for semantic segmentation. Specifically, we propose the Attention-to-Mask (ATM) module, in which the similarity maps between a set of learnable class tokens and the spatial feature maps are transferred to the segmentation masks. Experiments show that our proposed SegVit using the ATM module outperforms its counterparts using the plain ViT backbone on the ADE20K dataset and achieves new state-of-the-art performance on COCO-Stuff-10K and PASCAL-Context datasets. Furthermore, to reduce the computational cost of the ViT backbone, we propose query-based down-sampling (QD) and query-based up-sampling (QU) to build a Shrunk structure. With the proposed Shrunk structure, the model can save up to $40\%$ computations while maintaining competitive performance.
summary: We explore the capability of plain Vision Transformers (ViTs) for semantic segmentation and propose the SegVit. Previous ViT-based segmentation networks usually learn a pixel-level representation from the output of the ViT. Differently, we make use of the fundamental component -- attention mechanism, to generate masks for semantic segmentation. Specifically, we propose the Attention-to-Mask (ATM) module, in which the similarity maps between a set of learnable class tokens and the spatial feature maps are transferred to the segmentation masks. Experiments show that our proposed SegVit using the ATM module outperforms its counterparts using the plain ViT backbone on the ADE20K dataset and achieves new state-of-the-art performance on COCO-Stuff-10K and PASCAL-Context datasets. Furthermore, to reduce the computational cost of the ViT backbone, we propose query-based down-sampling (QD) and query-based up-sampling (QU) to build a Shrunk structure. With the proposed Shrunk structure, the model can save up to $40\%$ computations while maintaining competitive performance.
abstract_zh: 我们探索了普通视觉变换器（ViT）用于语义分割的能力，并提出了 SegVit。以前基于 ViT 的分割网络通常从 ViT 的输出中学习像素级表示。不同的是，我们利用基本组件——注意力机制，来生成语义分割的掩码。具体来说，我们提出了注意力掩模（ATM）模块，其中一组可学习的类标记和空间特征图之间的相似性图被转移到分割掩模。实验表明，我们提出的使用 ATM 模块的 SegVit 在 ADE20K 数据集上优于使用普通 ViT 主干的同行，并在 COCO-Stuff-10K 和 PASCAL-Context 数据集上实现了新的最先进的性能。此外，为了降低 ViT 主干的计算成本，我们提出基于查询的下采样（QD）和基于查询的上采样（QU）来构建 Shrunk 结构。通过建议的 Shrunk 结构，该模型可以节省高达 40\%$ 的计算量，同时保持具有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SegViT，利用视觉Transformer（ViT）的注意力机制进行语义分割，通过引入Attention-to-Mask（ATM）模块和计算优化结构提升性能。
AI.motivation: 传统ViT在分割任务中依赖像素级表示，未能充分利用注意力机制的潜力。本文旨在通过直接利用注意力生成分割掩码，提升分割效果并降低计算成本。
AI.method: 设计ATM模块，将可学习的类别标记与空间特征图的相似性映射转化为分割掩码；提出基于查询的下采样（QD）和上采样（QU）构建压缩结构，减少计算量。
AI.result: 在ADE20K数据集上优于同类方法，在COCO-Stuff-10K和PASCAL-Context上达到SOTA；压缩结构减少40%计算量且保持竞争力。
AI.conclusion: SegViT通过注意力机制和结构优化，在分割精度与效率上均优于现有方法，为ViT在分割任务中的应用提供了新思路。

authors_text: Bagus Tris Atmaja,Zanjabila,Suyanto,Akira Sasou
title: Cross-dataset COVID-19 Transfer Learning with Cough Detection, Cough Segmentation, and Data Augmentation
title_zh: 具有咳嗽检测、咳嗽分割和数据增强功能的跨数据集 COVID-19 迁移学习
abstract: This paper addresses issues on cough-based COVID-19 detection. We propose a cross-dataset transfer learning approach to improve the performance of COVID-19 detection by incorporating cough detection, cough segmentation, and data augmentation. The first aimed at removing non-cough signals and cough signals with low probability. The second aimed at segregating several coughs in a waveform into individual coughs. The third aimed at increasing the number of samples for the deep learning model. These three processing blocks are important as our finding revealed a large margin of improvement relative to the baseline methods without these blocks. An ablation study is conducted to optimize hyperparameters and it was found that alpha mixup is an important factor among others in improving the model performance via this augmentation method. A summary of this study with previous studies on the same evaluation set was given to gain insights into different methods of cough-based COVID-19 detection.
summary: This paper addresses issues on cough-based COVID-19 detection. We propose a cross-dataset transfer learning approach to improve the performance of COVID-19 detection by incorporating cough detection, cough segmentation, and data augmentation. The first aimed at removing non-cough signals and cough signals with low probability. The second aimed at segregating several coughs in a waveform into individual coughs. The third aimed at increasing the number of samples for the deep learning model. These three processing blocks are important as our finding revealed a large margin of improvement relative to the baseline methods without these blocks. An ablation study is conducted to optimize hyperparameters and it was found that alpha mixup is an important factor among others in improving the model performance via this augmentation method. A summary of this study with previous studies on the same evaluation set was given to gain insights into different methods of cough-based COVID-19 detection.
abstract_zh: 本文讨论了基于咳嗽的 COVID-19 检测问题。我们提出了一种跨数据集迁移学习方法，通过结合咳嗽检测、咳嗽分割和数据增强来提高 COVID-19 检测的性能。第一个旨在去除非咳嗽信号和低概率的咳嗽信号。第二个目标是将波形中的几次咳嗽分成单独的咳嗽。第三个目标是增加深度学习模型的样本数量。这三个处理块很重要，因为我们的发现表明，相对于没有这些块的基线方法，有很大的改进。进行了消融研究来优化超参数，发现 alpha mixup 是通过这种增强方法提高模型性能的一个重要因素。本研究与之前对同一评估集的研究进行了总结，以深入了解基于咳嗽的 COVID-19 检测的不同方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种跨数据集的迁移学习方法，通过咳嗽检测、咳嗽分割和数据增强技术提升基于咳嗽的COVID-19检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于咳嗽的COVID-19检测方法在跨数据集场景下表现不足，需通过优化预处理和增强技术提高模型泛化能力。
AI.method: 整合咳嗽检测（过滤非咳嗽信号）、咳嗽分割（分离多咳声信号）和数据增强（如AlphaMixup）三阶段处理流程，并通过消融实验优化超参数。
AI.result: 实验表明，三阶段处理较基线方法提升显著，其中AlphaMixup数据增强对性能提升贡献最大。
AI.conclusion: 跨数据集迁移学习框架结合多阶段信号处理与增强技术能有效提升咳嗽声纹的COVID-19检测准确率，为后续研究提供方法参考。

We propose a cross-dataset transfer learning approach to improve the performance of COVID-19 detection by incorporating cough detection, cough segmentation, and data augmentation. The first aimed at removing non-cough signals and cough signals with low probability. The second aimed at segregating several coughs in a waveform into individual coughs. The third aimed at increasing the number of samples for the deep learning model. These three processing blocks are important as our finding revealed a large margin of improvement relative to the baseline methods without these blocks. An ablation study is conducted to optimize hyperparameters and it was found that alpha mixup is an important factor among others in improving the model performance via this augmentation method. A summary of this study with previous studies on the same evaluation set was given to gain insights into different methods of cough-based COVID-19 detection. 本文讨论了基于咳嗽的 COVID-19 检测问题。我们提出了一种跨数据集迁移学习方法，通过结合咳嗽检测、咳嗽分割和数据增强来提高 COVID-19 检测的性能。第一个旨在去除非咳嗽信号和低概率的咳嗽信号。第二个目标是将波形中的几次咳嗽分成单独的咳嗽。第三个目标是增加深度学习模型的样本数量。这三个处理块很重要，因为我们的发现表明，相对于没有这些块的基线方法，有很大的改进。进行了消融研究来优化超参数，发现 alpha mixup 是通过这种增强方法提高模型性能的一个重要因素。本研究与之前对同一评估集的研究进行了总结，以深入了解基于咳嗽的 COVID-19 检测的不同方法。 本文提出了一种跨数据集的迁移学习方法，通过咳嗽检测、咳嗽分割和数据增强技术提升基于咳嗽的COVID-19检测性能。 Bagus Tris Atmaja,Zanjabila,Suyanto,Akira Sasou eess.AS,cs.SD

authors_text: Jielin Qiu,Franck Dernoncourt,Trung Bui,Zhaowen Wang,Ding Zhao,Hailin Jin
title: LiveSeg: Unsupervised Multimodal Temporal Segmentation of Long Livestream Videos
title_zh: LiveSeg：长直播视频的无监督多模态时间分割
abstract: Livestream videos have become a significant part of online learning, where design, digital marketing, creative painting, and other skills are taught by experienced experts in the sessions, making them valuable materials. However, Livestream tutorial videos are usually hours long, recorded, and uploaded to the Internet directly after the live sessions, making it hard for other people to catch up quickly. An outline will be a beneficial solution, which requires the video to be temporally segmented according to topics. In this work, we introduced a large Livestream video dataset named MultiLive, and formulated the temporal segmentation of the long Livestream videos (TSLLV) task. We propose LiveSeg, an unsupervised Livestream video temporal Segmentation solution, which takes advantage of multimodal features from different domains. Our method achieved a $16.8\%$ F1-score performance improvement compared with the state-of-the-art method.
summary: Livestream videos have become a significant part of online learning, where design, digital marketing, creative painting, and other skills are taught by experienced experts in the sessions, making them valuable materials. However, Livestream tutorial videos are usually hours long, recorded, and uploaded to the Internet directly after the live sessions, making it hard for other people to catch up quickly. An outline will be a beneficial solution, which requires the video to be temporally segmented according to topics. In this work, we introduced a large Livestream video dataset named MultiLive, and formulated the temporal segmentation of the long Livestream videos (TSLLV) task. We propose LiveSeg, an unsupervised Livestream video temporal Segmentation solution, which takes advantage of multimodal features from different domains. Our method achieved a $16.8\%$ F1-score performance improvement compared with the state-of-the-art method.
abstract_zh: 直播视频已成为在线学习的重要组成部分，经验丰富的专家在课程中教授设计、数字营销、创意绘画和其他技能，使其成为宝贵的材料。然而，直播教程视频通常长达数小时，并在直播结束后直接录制并上传到互联网，这使得其他人很难快速跟上。大纲将是一个有益的解决方案，它需要根据主题对视频进行时间分段。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个名为 MultiLive 的大型直播视频数据集，并制定了长直播视频（TSLLV）任务的时间分割。我们提出了 LiveSeg，一种无监督的直播视频时间分割解决方案，它利用了来自不同领域的多模态特征。与最先进的方法相比，我们的方法实现了 $16.8\%$ F1 分数性能改进。

AI.tldr: 提出一种无监督多模态方法LiveSeg，用于对长直播视频进行时序分割，生成主题大纲。
AI.motivation: 直播教学视频通常时长较长，直接上传后难以快速浏览，需要按主题进行分割以生成大纲，提高学习效率。
AI.method: 提出LiveSeg方法，结合多模态特征（如视觉、音频、文本等），在无监督条件下对长直播视频进行时序分割。
AI.result: 相比现有方法，LiveSeg在F1分数上提升了16.8%。
AI.conclusion: LiveSeg有效提升了长直播视频分割性能，同时MultiLive数据集为相关研究提供了支持。

search_text: LiveSeg: Unsupervised Multimodal Temporal Segmentation of Long Livestream Videos LiveSeg：长直播视频的无监督多模态时间分割 Livestream videos have become a significant part of online learning, where design, digital marketing, creative painting, and other skills are taught by experienced experts in the sessions, making them valuable materials. However, Livestream tutorial videos are usually hours long, recorded, and uploaded to the Internet directly after the live sessions, making it hard for other people to catch up quickly. An outline will be a beneficial solution, which requires the video to be temporally segmented according to topics. In this work, we introduced a large Livestream video dataset named MultiLive, and formulated the temporal segmentation of the long Livestream videos (TSLLV) task. We propose LiveSeg, an unsupervised Livestream video temporal Segmentation solution, which takes advantage of multimodal features from different domains. Our method achieved a $16.8\%$ F1-score performance improvement compared with the state-of-the-art method. 直播视频已成为在线学习的重要组成部分，经验丰富的专家在课程中教授设计、数字营销、创意绘画和其他技能，使其成为宝贵的材料。然而，直播教程视频通常长达数小时，并在直播结束后直接录制并上传到互联网，这使得其他人很难快速跟上。大纲将是一个有益的解决方案，它需要根据主题对视频进行时间分段。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个名为 MultiLive 的大型直播视频数据集，并制定了长直播视频（TSLLV）任务的时间分割。我们提出了 LiveSeg，一种无监督的直播视频时间分割解决方案，它利用了来自不同领域的多模态特征。与最先进的方法相比，我们的方法实现了 $16.8\%$ F1 分数性能改进。 提出一种无监督多模态方法LiveSeg，用于对长直播视频进行时序分割，生成主题大纲。 Jielin Qiu,Franck Dernoncourt,Trung Bui,Zhaowen Wang,Ding Zhao,Hailin Jin cs.CV

authors_text: Nazneen Rajani,Weixin Liang,Lingjiao Chen,Meg Mitchell,James Zou
title: SEAL : Interactive Tool for Systematic Error Analysis and Labeling
title_zh: SEAL：用于系统错误分析和标记的交互式工具
abstract: With the advent of Transformers, large language models (LLMs) have saturated well-known NLP benchmarks and leaderboards with high aggregate performance. However, many times these models systematically fail on tail data or rare groups not obvious in aggregate evaluation. Identifying such problematic data groups is even more challenging when there are no explicit labels (e.g., ethnicity, gender, etc.) and further compounded for NLP datasets due to the lack of visual features to characterize failure modes (e.g., Asian males, animals indoors, waterbirds on land, etc.). This paper introduces an interactive Systematic Error Analysis and Labeling (\seal) tool that uses a two-step approach to first identify high error slices of data and then, in the second step, introduce methods to give human-understandable semantics to those underperforming slices. We explore a variety of methods for coming up with coherent semantics for the error groups using language models for semantic labeling and a text-to-image model for generating visual features. SEAL toolkit and demo screencast is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/nazneen/seal.
summary: With the advent of Transformers, large language models (LLMs) have saturated well-known NLP benchmarks and leaderboards with high aggregate performance. However, many times these models systematically fail on tail data or rare groups not obvious in aggregate evaluation. Identifying such problematic data groups is even more challenging when there are no explicit labels (e.g., ethnicity, gender, etc.) and further compounded for NLP datasets due to the lack of visual features to characterize failure modes (e.g., Asian males, animals indoors, waterbirds on land, etc.). This paper introduces an interactive Systematic Error Analysis and Labeling (\seal) tool that uses a two-step approach to first identify high error slices of data and then, in the second step, introduce methods to give human-understandable semantics to those underperforming slices. We explore a variety of methods for coming up with coherent semantics for the error groups using language models for semantic labeling and a text-to-image model for generating visual features. SEAL toolkit and demo screencast is available at https://huggingface.co/spaces/nazneen/seal.
abstract_zh: 随着 Transformers 的出现，大型语言模型 (LLM) 已经以较高的综合性能饱和了著名的 NLP 基准测试和排行榜。然而，很多时候这些模型在尾部数据或在总体评估中不明显的稀有群体上系统性地失败。当没有明确的标签（例如种族、性别等）并且由于缺乏表征故障模式的视觉特征（例如亚洲男性、室内动物、陆地上的水鸟等）而导致 NLP 数据集进一步复杂化时，识别此类有问题的数据组甚至更具挑战性。本文介绍了一种交互式系统错误分析和标记（\seal）工具，该工具使用两步方法首先识别高错误数据切片，然后在第二步中引入方法，为那些表现不佳的切片提供人类可理解的语义。我们探索了多种方法，使用用于语义标记的语言模型和用于生成视觉特征的文本到图像模型来为错误组提供连贯的语义。 SEAL 工具包和演示截屏视频可在 https://huggingface.co/spaces/nazneen/seal 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SEAL的交互式工具，用于系统性地分析和标注大型语言模型在罕见数据组或尾部数据上的系统性错误。
AI.motivation: 尽管大型语言模型在主流NLP基准测试中表现优异，但它们在罕见群体或尾部数据上常出现系统性失败，而这些数据缺乏显式标签（如性别、种族）且难以通过视觉特征表征。
AI.method: SEAL采用两步法：首先识别高错误率的数据切片，然后利用语言模型生成语义标签，并结合文本到图像模型生成视觉特征，为这些切片赋予人类可理解的语义。
AI.result: SEAL工具成功识别了模型在罕见数据上的系统性错误，并通过语义标签和视觉特征生成方法为错误切片提供了可解释的描述。
AI.conclusion: SEAL为分析和标注NLP模型在无显式标签的罕见数据上的系统性错误提供了交互式解决方案，有助于提升模型的公平性和性能。

authors_text: Peiye Zhuang,Bliss Chapman,Ran Li,Oluwasanmi Koyejo
title: Synthetic Power Analyses: Empirical Evaluation and Application to Cognitive Neuroimaging
title_zh: 综合功效分析：认知神经影像的实证评估和应用
abstract: In the experimental sciences, statistical power analyses are often used before data collection to determine the required sample size. However, traditional power analyses can be costly when data are difficult or expensive to collect. We propose synthetic power analyses; a framework for estimating statistical power at various sample sizes, and empirically explore the performance of synthetic power analysis for sample size selection in cognitive neuroscience experiments. To this end, brain imaging data is synthesized using an implicit generative model conditioned on observed cognitive processes. Further, we propose a simple procedure to modify the statistical tests which result in conservative statistics. Our empirical results suggest that synthetic power analysis could be a low-cost alternative to pilot data collection when the proposed experiments share cognitive processes with previously conducted experiments.
summary: In the experimental sciences, statistical power analyses are often used before data collection to determine the required sample size. However, traditional power analyses can be costly when data are difficult or expensive to collect. We propose synthetic power analyses; a framework for estimating statistical power at various sample sizes, and empirically explore the performance of synthetic power analysis for sample size selection in cognitive neuroscience experiments. To this end, brain imaging data is synthesized using an implicit generative model conditioned on observed cognitive processes. Further, we propose a simple procedure to modify the statistical tests which result in conservative statistics. Our empirical results suggest that synthetic power analysis could be a low-cost alternative to pilot data collection when the proposed experiments share cognitive processes with previously conducted experiments.
abstract_zh: 在实验科学中，通常在数据收集之前使用统计功效分析来确定所需的样本量。然而，当数据收集困难或成本高昂时，传统功率分析的成本可能会很高。我们提出综合功率分析；用于估计不同样本量的统计功效的框架，并凭经验探索认知神经科学实验中样本量选择的合成功效分析的性能。为此，使用以观察到的认知过程为条件的隐式生成模型来合成大脑成像数据。此外，我们提出了一个简单的程序来修改导致保守统计的统计测试。我们的实证结果表明，当所提出的实验与之前进行的实验共享认知过程时，合成功效分析可能是试点数据收集的低成本替代方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种合成功效分析框架，用于在认知神经科学实验中估计统计功效，作为传统方法的低成本替代方案。
AI.motivation: 传统统计功效分析在数据难以收集或成本高昂时效率低下，需寻找基于已有认知过程的经济替代方法。
AI.method: 利用隐式生成模型合成脑成像数据，并调整统计检验以生成保守统计量，评估不同样本量的统计功效。
AI.result: 实证表明，合成功效分析在共享认知过程的实验中能有效替代传统试点数据收集，显著降低成本。
AI.conclusion: 合成功效分析可作为特定条件下传统方法的可行替代，减少对试点数据的依赖。

To this end, brain imaging data is synthesized using an implicit generative model conditioned on observed cognitive processes. Further, we propose a simple procedure to modify the statistical tests which result in conservative statistics. Our empirical results suggest that synthetic power analysis could be a low-cost alternative to pilot data collection when the proposed experiments share cognitive processes with previously conducted experiments. 在实验科学中，通常在数据收集之前使用统计功效分析来确定所需的样本量。然而，当数据收集困难或成本高昂时，传统功率分析的成本可能会很高。我们提出综合功率分析；用于估计不同样本量的统计功效的框架，并凭经验探索认知神经科学实验中样本量选择的合成功效分析的性能。为此，使用以观察到的认知过程为条件的隐式生成模型来合成大脑成像数据。此外，我们提出了一个简单的程序来修改导致保守统计的统计测试。我们的实证结果表明，当所提出的实验与之前进行的实验共享认知过程时，合成功效分析可能是试点数据收集的低成本替代方案。 本文提出了一种合成功效分析框架，用于在认知神经科学实验中估计统计功效，作为传统方法的低成本替代方案。 Peiye Zhuang,Bliss Chapman,Ran Li,Oluwasanmi Koyejo cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Nidhin Harilal,Rohan Patil
title: Effectiveness of the Recent Advances in Capsule Networks
title_zh: 胶囊网络最新进展的有效性
abstract: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have revolutionized the field of deep neural networks. However, recent research has shown that CNNs fail to generalize under various conditions and hence the idea of capsules was introduced in 2011, though the real surge of research started from 2017. In this paper, we present an overview of the recent advances in capsule architecture and routing mechanisms. In addition, we find that the relative focus in recent literature is on modifying routing procedure or architecture as a whole but the study of other finer components, specifically, squash function is wanting. Thus, we also present some new insights regarding the effect of squash functions in performance of the capsule networks. Finally, we conclude by discussing and proposing possible opportunities in the field of capsule networks.
summary: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have revolutionized the field of deep neural networks. However, recent research has shown that CNNs fail to generalize under various conditions and hence the idea of capsules was introduced in 2011, though the real surge of research started from 2017. In this paper, we present an overview of the recent advances in capsule architecture and routing mechanisms. In addition, we find that the relative focus in recent literature is on modifying routing procedure or architecture as a whole but the study of other finer components, specifically, squash function is wanting. Thus, we also present some new insights regarding the effect of squash functions in performance of the capsule networks. Finally, we conclude by discussing and proposing possible opportunities in the field of capsule networks.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络（CNN）彻底改变了深度神经网络领域。然而，最近的研究表明，CNN 在各种条件下都无法泛化，因此胶囊的概念在 2011 年被引入，尽管真正的研究浪潮是从 2017 年开始的。在本文中，我们概述了胶囊架构和路由机制的最新进展。此外，我们发现最近文献的相对焦点是修改整个路由过程或体系结构，但缺乏对其他更精细组件（特别是挤压功能）的研究。因此，我们还提出了一些关于挤压函数对胶囊网络性能影响的新见解。最后，我们通过讨论并提出胶囊网络领域可能的机会来结束。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了胶囊网络的最新进展，并探讨了挤压函数对网络性能的影响。
AI.motivation: 由于CNN在泛化能力上的不足，胶囊网络被提出，但现有研究多关注路由机制和架构，而对挤压函数等细节研究不足。
AI.method: 通过综述近期胶囊网络架构和路由机制的研究，并分析挤压函数在其中的作用。
AI.result: 发现挤压函数对胶囊网络性能有显著影响，并提出了新的优化见解。
AI.conclusion: 指出未来研究可进一步探索挤压函数及其他组件的优化，以提升胶囊网络性能。

authors_text: Ling Li,David Thorsley,Joseph Hassoun
title: SaiT: Sparse Vision Transformers through Adaptive Token Pruning
title_zh: SaiT：通过自适应令牌修剪的稀疏视觉变压器
abstract: While vision transformers have achieved impressive results, effectively and efficiently accelerating these models can further boost performances. In this work, we propose a dense/sparse training framework to obtain a unified model, enabling weight sharing across various token densities. Thus one model offers a range of accuracy and throughput tradeoffs for different applications. Besides, we introduce adaptive token pruning to optimize the patch token sparsity based on the input image. In addition, we investigate knowledge distillation to enhance token selection capability in early transformer modules. Sparse adaptive image Transformer (SaiT) offers varying levels of model acceleration by merely changing the token sparsity on the fly. Specifically, SaiT reduces the computation complexity (FLOPs) by 39% - 43% and increases the throughput by 67% - 91% with less than 0.5% accuracy loss for various vision transformer models. Meanwhile, the same model also provides the zero accuracy drop option by skipping the sparsification step. SaiT achieves better accuracy and computation tradeoffs than state-of-the-art transformer and convolutional models.
summary: While vision transformers have achieved impressive results, effectively and efficiently accelerating these models can further boost performances. In this work, we propose a dense/sparse training framework to obtain a unified model, enabling weight sharing across various token densities. Thus one model offers a range of accuracy and throughput tradeoffs for different applications. Besides, we introduce adaptive token pruning to optimize the patch token sparsity based on the input image. In addition, we investigate knowledge distillation to enhance token selection capability in early transformer modules. Sparse adaptive image Transformer (SaiT) offers varying levels of model acceleration by merely changing the token sparsity on the fly. Specifically, SaiT reduces the computation complexity (FLOPs) by 39% - 43% and increases the throughput by 67% - 91% with less than 0.5% accuracy loss for various vision transformer models. Meanwhile, the same model also provides the zero accuracy drop option by skipping the sparsification step. SaiT achieves better accuracy and computation tradeoffs than state-of-the-art transformer and convolutional models.
abstract_zh: 虽然视觉转换器已经取得了令人印象深刻的成果，但有效且高效地加速这些模型可以进一步提高性能。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个密集/稀疏训练框架来获得统一的模型，从而实现跨各种令牌密度的权重共享。因此，一个模型为不同的应用提供了一系列精度和吞吐量的权衡。此外，我们引入自适应令牌剪枝来根据输入图像优化补丁令牌稀疏性。此外，我们研究知识蒸馏以增强早期变压器模块中的令牌选择能力。稀疏自适应图像 Transformer (SaiT) 仅通过动态更改令牌稀疏性即可提供不同级别的模型加速。具体来说，SaiT 将计算复杂度 (FLOP) 降低了 39% - 43%，吞吐量提高了 67% - 91%，并且各种视觉变换器模型的精度损失不到 0.5%。同时，同一模型还通过跳过稀疏化步骤提供零精度下降选项。 SaiT 比最先进的变压器和卷积模型实现了更好的准确性和计算权衡。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SaiT方法，通过自适应令牌剪枝和统一训练框架加速视觉Transformer模型。
AI.motivation: 视觉Transformer虽性能优异但计算量大，需在保持精度的前提下提升效率以适应不同应用场景。
AI.method: 设计密集/稀疏联合训练框架实现权重共享，结合输入自适应令牌剪枝与知识蒸馏优化早期模块的令牌选择能力。
AI.result: 在多个视觉Transformer模型上实现39%-43%的计算量降低和67%-91%的吞吐量提升，精度损失小于0.5%且支持零精度下降模式。
AI.conclusion: SaiT在计算效率与精度的权衡上优于现有Transformer和卷积模型，提供灵活的模型加速方案。

authors_text: Ali Omrani,Brendan Kennedy,Mohammad Atari,Morteza Dehghani
title: Social-Group-Agnostic Word Embedding Debiasing via the Stereotype Content Model
title_zh: 通过刻板印象内容模型消除与社会群体无关的词嵌入偏差
abstract: Existing word embedding debiasing methods require social-group-specific word pairs (e.g., "man"-"woman") for each social attribute (e.g., gender), which cannot be used to mitigate bias for other social groups, making these methods impractical or costly to incorporate understudied social groups in debiasing. We propose that the Stereotype Content Model (SCM), a theoretical framework developed in social psychology for understanding the content of stereotypes, which structures stereotype content along two psychological dimensions - "warmth" and "competence" - can help debiasing efforts to become social-group-agnostic by capturing the underlying connection between bias and stereotypes. Using only pairs of terms for warmth (e.g., "genuine"-"fake") and competence (e.g.,"smart"-"stupid"), we perform debiasing with established methods and find that, across gender, race, and age, SCM-based debiasing performs comparably to group-specific debiasing
summary: Existing word embedding debiasing methods require social-group-specific word pairs (e.g., "man"-"woman") for each social attribute (e.g., gender), which cannot be used to mitigate bias for other social groups, making these methods impractical or costly to incorporate understudied social groups in debiasing. We propose that the Stereotype Content Model (SCM), a theoretical framework developed in social psychology for understanding the content of stereotypes, which structures stereotype content along two psychological dimensions - "warmth" and "competence" - can help debiasing efforts to become social-group-agnostic by capturing the underlying connection between bias and stereotypes. Using only pairs of terms for warmth (e.g., "genuine"-"fake") and competence (e.g.,"smart"-"stupid"), we perform debiasing with established methods and find that, across gender, race, and age, SCM-based debiasing performs comparably to group-specific debiasing
abstract_zh: 现有的词嵌入去偏见方法需要针对每个社会属性（例如性别）的特定于社会群体的词对（例如“男人”-“女人”），这不能用于减轻对其他社会群体的偏见，使得这些方法在去偏见中纳入未充分研究的社会群体是不切实际的或成本高昂的。我们提出，刻板印象内容模型（SCM）是社会心理学中为理解刻板印象内容而开发的理论框架，它沿着两个心理维度（“温暖”和“能力”）构建刻板印象内容，可以通过捕捉偏见和刻板印象之间的潜在联系来帮助消除偏见，从而变得与社会群体无关。仅使用温暖（例如“真正”-“假”）和能力（例如“聪明”-“愚蠢”）的术语对，我们使用既定方法进行去偏，并发现，在性别、种族和年龄上，基于 SCM 的去偏与特定群体的去偏表现相当。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于刻板印象内容模型（SCM）的无社会群体依赖的词嵌入去偏方法，通过温暖和能力两个维度实现跨群体的去偏效果。
AI.motivation: 现有去偏方法需针对每个社会群体（如性别、种族）定制特定词对，难以扩展至未被研究的群体，且成本高昂。
AI.method: 利用SCM理论框架中‘温暖’（如‘真诚-虚假’）和‘能力’（如‘聪明-愚蠢’）两个心理维度，采用通用词对进行去偏，无需群体特异性输入。
AI.result: 在性别、种族和年龄等多维度测试中，SCM去偏方法效果与群体特异性方法相当，证明其跨群体有效性。
AI.conclusion: SCM模型为词嵌入去偏提供了通用解决方案，通过心理维度关联偏见与刻板印象，显著降低对群体特异性数据的依赖。

ai_content_json.result: 在性别、种族和年龄等多维度测试中，SCM去偏方法效果与群体特异性方法相当，证明其跨群体有效性。
ai_content_json.conclusion: SCM模型为词嵌入去偏提供了通用解决方案，通过心理维度关联偏见与刻板印象，显著降低对群体特异性数据的依赖。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于刻板印象内容模型（SCM）的无社会群体依赖的词嵌入去偏方法，通过温暖和能力两个维度实现跨群体的去偏效果。
motivation: 现有去偏方法需针对每个社会群体（如性别、种族）定制特定词对，难以扩展至未被研究的群体，且成本高昂。
method: 利用SCM理论框架中‘温暖’（如‘真诚-虚假’）和‘能力’（如‘聪明-愚蠢’）两个心理维度，采用通用词对进行去偏，无需群体特异性输入。
result: 在性别、种族和年龄等多维度测试中，SCM去偏方法效果与群体特异性方法相当，证明其跨群体有效性。
conclusion: SCM模型为词嵌入去偏提供了通用解决方案，通过心理维度关联偏见与刻板印象，显著降低对群体特异性数据的依赖。
search_text: Social-Group-Agnostic Word Embedding Debiasing via the Stereotype Content Model 通过刻板印象内容模型消除与社会群体无关的词嵌入偏差 Existing word embedding debiasing methods require social-group-specific word pairs (e.g., "man"-"woman") for each social attribute (e.g., gender), which cannot be used to mitigate bias for other social groups, making these methods impractical or costly to incorporate understudied social groups in debiasing. We propose that the Stereotype Content Model (SCM), a theoretical framework developed in social psychology for understanding the content of stereotypes, which structures stereotype content along two psychological dimensions - "warmth" and "competence" - can help debiasing efforts to become social-group-agnostic by capturing the underlying connection between bias and stereotypes. Using only pairs of terms for warmth (e.g., "genuine"-"fake") and competence (e.g.,"smart"-"stupid"), we perform debiasing with established methods and find that, across gender, race, and age, SCM-based debiasing performs comparably to group-specific debiasing 现有的词嵌入去偏见方法需要针对每个社会属性（例如性别）的特定于社会群体的词对（例如“男人”-“女人”），这不能用于减轻对其他社会群体的偏见，使得这些方法在去偏见中纳入未充分研究的社会群体是不切实际的或成本高昂的。我们提出，刻板印象内容模型（SCM）是社会心理学中为理解刻板印象内容而开发的理论框架，它沿着两个心理维度（“温暖”和“能力”）构建刻板印象内容，可以通过捕捉偏见和刻板印象之间的潜在联系来帮助消除偏见，从而变得与社会群体无关。仅使用温暖（例如“真正”-“假”）和能力（例如“聪明”-“愚蠢”）的术语对，我们使用既定方法进行去偏，并发现，在性别、种族和年龄上，基于 SCM 的去偏与特定群体的去偏表现相当。 本文提出一种基于刻板印象内容模型（SCM）的无社会群体依赖的词嵌入去偏方法，通过温暖和能力两个维度实现跨群体的去偏效果。 Ali Omrani,Brendan Kennedy,Mohammad Atari,Morteza Dehghani cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Peiye Zhuang,Jia-bin Huang,Ayush Saraf,Xuejian Rong,Changil Kim,Denis Demandolx
title: AMICO: Amodal Instance Composition
title_zh: AMICO：Amodal 实例组合
abstract: Image composition aims to blend multiple objects to form a harmonized image. Existing approaches often assume precisely segmented and intact objects. Such assumptions, however, are hard to satisfy in unconstrained scenarios. We present Amodal Instance Composition for compositing imperfect -- potentially incomplete and/or coarsely segmented -- objects onto a target image. We first develop object shape prediction and content completion modules to synthesize the amodal contents. We then propose a neural composition model to blend the objects seamlessly. Our primary technical novelty lies in using separate foreground/background representations and blending mask prediction to alleviate segmentation errors. Our results show state-of-the-art performance on public COCOA and KINS benchmarks and attain favorable visual results across diverse scenes. We demonstrate various image composition applications such as object insertion and de-occlusion.
summary: Image composition aims to blend multiple objects to form a harmonized image. Existing approaches often assume precisely segmented and intact objects. Such assumptions, however, are hard to satisfy in unconstrained scenarios. We present Amodal Instance Composition for compositing imperfect -- potentially incomplete and/or coarsely segmented -- objects onto a target image. We first develop object shape prediction and content completion modules to synthesize the amodal contents. We then propose a neural composition model to blend the objects seamlessly. Our primary technical novelty lies in using separate foreground/background representations and blending mask prediction to alleviate segmentation errors. Our results show state-of-the-art performance on public COCOA and KINS benchmarks and attain favorable visual results across diverse scenes. We demonstrate various image composition applications such as object insertion and de-occlusion.
abstract_zh: 图像合成旨在混合多个对象以形成协调的图像。现有的方法通常假设精确分割且完整的对象。然而，在不受约束的情况下，这样的假设很难满足。我们提出了 Amodal Instance Composition，用于将不完美（可能不完整和/或粗略分割）的对象合成到目标图像上。我们首先开发对象形状预测和内容完成模块来合成非模态内容。然后，我们提出了一种神经组合模型来无缝地混合对象。我们的主要技术新颖性在于使用单独的前景/背景表示和混合掩模预测来减轻分割错误。我们的结果显示了公共 COCOA 和 KINS 基准上最先进的性能，并在不同场景中获得了良好的视觉效果。我们演示了各种图像合成应用，例如对象插入和去遮挡。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AMICO方法，解决图像合成中不完美物体（不完整或粗略分割）的合成问题。
AI.motivation: 现有图像合成方法依赖精确分割的完整物体，但在实际场景中难以满足这一假设，需处理不完美物体的合成。
AI.method: 开发物体形状预测与内容完成模块生成缺失部分，通过分离的前景/背景表示及混合掩码预测，结合神经合成模型实现无缝融合。
AI.result: 在COCOA和KINS基准上达到SOTA性能，视觉效果优于现有方法，并成功应用于物体插入与去遮挡任务。
AI.conclusion: AMICO通过处理分割误差显著提升了复杂场景下的图像合成质量，为实际应用提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Peiye Zhuang,Liqian Ma,Oluwasanmi Koyejo,Alexander G. Schwing
title: Controllable Radiance Fields for Dynamic Face Synthesis
title_zh: 用于动态人脸合成的可控辐射场
abstract: Recent work on 3D-aware image synthesis has achieved compelling results using advances in neural rendering. However, 3D-aware synthesis of face dynamics hasn't received much attention. Here, we study how to explicitly control generative model synthesis of face dynamics exhibiting non-rigid motion (e.g., facial expression change), while simultaneously ensuring 3D-awareness. For this we propose a Controllable Radiance Field (CoRF): 1) Motion control is achieved by embedding motion features within the layered latent motion space of a style-based generator; 2) To ensure consistency of background, motion features and subject-specific attributes such as lighting, texture, shapes, albedo, and identity, a face parsing net, a head regressor and an identity encoder are incorporated. On head image/video data we show that CoRFs are 3D-aware while enabling editing of identity, viewing directions, and motion.
summary: Recent work on 3D-aware image synthesis has achieved compelling results using advances in neural rendering. However, 3D-aware synthesis of face dynamics hasn't received much attention. Here, we study how to explicitly control generative model synthesis of face dynamics exhibiting non-rigid motion (e.g., facial expression change), while simultaneously ensuring 3D-awareness. For this we propose a Controllable Radiance Field (CoRF): 1) Motion control is achieved by embedding motion features within the layered latent motion space of a style-based generator; 2) To ensure consistency of background, motion features and subject-specific attributes such as lighting, texture, shapes, albedo, and identity, a face parsing net, a head regressor and an identity encoder are incorporated. On head image/video data we show that CoRFs are 3D-aware while enabling editing of identity, viewing directions, and motion.
abstract_zh: 最近，利用神经渲染方面的进步，3D 感知图像合成方面的工作取得了引人注目的成果。然而，面部动态的 3D 感知合成并没有受到太多关注。在这里，我们研究如何显式控制表现出非刚性运动（例如面部表情变化）的面部动态生成模型合成，同时确保 3D 感知。为此，我们提出了可控辐射场（CoRF）：1）通过在基于样式的生成器的分层潜在运动空间中嵌入运动特征来实现运动控制； 2）为了确保背景、运动特征和特定主题属性（例如光照、纹理、形状、反照率和身份）的一致性，结合了面部解析网络、头部回归器和身份编码器。在头部图像/视频数据上，我们表明 CoRF 具有 3D 感知能力，同时能够编辑身份、观看方向和运动。

AI.tldr: 本文提出可控辐射场（CoRF），用于实现具有3D感知能力的动态人脸合成，能够显式控制非刚性运动（如面部表情变化）的生成。
AI.motivation: 尽管神经渲染在3D感知图像合成领域取得进展，但针对动态人脸（如非刚性运动）的3D感知合成研究不足，现有方法难以同时控制运动和保持3D一致性。
AI.method: 通过将运动特征嵌入基于风格的生成器的分层潜在运动空间，结合人脸解析网络、头部回归器和身份编码器，实现运动控制与背景、光照、纹理等属性的一致性。
AI.result: 在头部图像/视频数据上验证了CoRF的3D感知能力，可编辑身份、视角方向和运动，生成符合非刚性动态的人脸图像。
AI.conclusion: CoRF为动态人脸合成提供了可控且3D感知的解决方案，推动了面部表情、姿态等非刚性运动的高质量生成研究。

authors_text: Hyunchai Jeong,Jin Tian,Elias Bareinboim
title: Finding and Listing Front-door Adjustment Sets
title_zh: 查找并列出前门调节套件
comment: Pages: 18 (main paper 10, references 2, appendix 6), Figures: 9 (main paper 7, appendix 2), to be published in Proceedings of the 36th Annual Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems
comments: Pages: 18 (main paper 10, references 2, appendix 6), Figures: 9 (main paper 7, appendix 2), to be published in Proceedings of the 36th Annual Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems
abstract: Identifying the effects of new interventions from data is a significant challenge found across a wide range of the empirical sciences. A well-known strategy for identifying such effects is Pearl's front-door (FD) criterion (Pearl, 1995). The definition of the FD criterion is declarative, only allowing one to decide whether a specific set satisfies the criterion. In this paper, we present algorithms for finding and enumerating possible sets satisfying the FD criterion in a given causal diagram. These results are useful in facilitating the practical applications of the FD criterion for causal effects estimation and helping scientists to select estimands with desired properties, e.g., based on cost, feasibility of measurement, or statistical power.
summary: Identifying the effects of new interventions from data is a significant challenge found across a wide range of the empirical sciences. A well-known strategy for identifying such effects is Pearl's front-door (FD) criterion (Pearl, 1995). The definition of the FD criterion is declarative, only allowing one to decide whether a specific set satisfies the criterion. In this paper, we present algorithms for finding and enumerating possible sets satisfying the FD criterion in a given causal diagram. These results are useful in facilitating the practical applications of the FD criterion for causal effects estimation and helping scientists to select estimands with desired properties, e.g., based on cost, feasibility of measurement, or statistical power.
abstract_zh: 从数据中识别新干预措施的影响是广泛的实证科学领域面临的一项重大挑战。识别此类效应的一个著名策略是 Pearl 的前门 (FD) 标准（Pearl，1995）。 FD标准的定义是声明性的，仅允许人们决定特定集合是否满足该标准。在本文中，我们提出了在给定因果图中查找和枚举满足 FD 准则的可能集的算法。这些结果有助于促进因果效应估计的 FD 标准的实际应用，并帮助科学家选择具有所需属性的估计值，例如基于成本、测量的可行性或统计功效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出算法用于在因果图中寻找和列举满足前门准则的调整集，以辅助因果效应估计。
AI.motivation: 前门准则虽能识别干预效应，但其声明式定义无法直接用于寻找符合条件的调整集，限制了实际应用。
AI.method: 设计算法在给定因果图中系统搜索并枚举所有满足前门准则的调整集。
AI.result: 算法有效支持前门准则的实用化，帮助科学家基于成本、可行性等因素选择最优估计量。
AI.conclusion: 该方法填补了前门准则应用的算法空白，提升了因果效应估计的可操作性。

search_text: Finding and Listing Front-door Adjustment Sets 查找并列出前门调节套件 Identifying the effects of new interventions from data is a significant challenge found across a wide range of the empirical sciences. A well-known strategy for identifying such effects is Pearl's front-door (FD) criterion (Pearl, 1995). The definition of the FD criterion is declarative, only allowing one to decide whether a specific set satisfies the criterion. In this paper, we present algorithms for finding and enumerating possible sets satisfying the FD criterion in a given causal diagram. These results are useful in facilitating the practical applications of the FD criterion for causal effects estimation and helping scientists to select estimands with desired properties, e.g., based on cost, feasibility of measurement, or statistical power. 从数据中识别新干预措施的影响是广泛的实证科学领域面临的一项重大挑战。识别此类效应的一个著名策略是 Pearl 的前门 (FD) 标准（Pearl，1995）。 FD标准的定义是声明性的，仅允许人们决定特定集合是否满足该标准。在本文中，我们提出了在给定因果图中查找和枚举满足 FD 准则的可能集的算法。这些结果有助于促进因果效应估计的 FD 标准的实际应用，并帮助科学家选择具有所需属性的估计值，例如基于成本、测量的可行性或统计功效。 本文提出算法用于在因果图中寻找和列举满足前门准则的调整集，以辅助因果效应估计。 Hyunchai Jeong,Jin Tian,Elias Bareinboim stat.ME,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Ben Hutchinson,Jason Baldridge,Vinodkumar Prabhakaran
title: Underspecification in Scene Description-to-Depiction Tasks
title_zh: 场景描述到描述任务中的规范不足
abstract: Questions regarding implicitness, ambiguity and underspecification are crucial for understanding the task validity and ethical concerns of multimodal image+text systems, yet have received little attention to date. This position paper maps out a conceptual framework to address this gap, focusing on systems which generate images depicting scenes from scene descriptions. In doing so, we account for how texts and images convey meaning differently. We outline a set of core challenges concerning textual and visual ambiguity, as well as risks that may be amplified by ambiguous and underspecified elements. We propose and discuss strategies for addressing these challenges, including generating visually ambiguous images, and generating a set of diverse images.
summary: Questions regarding implicitness, ambiguity and underspecification are crucial for understanding the task validity and ethical concerns of multimodal image+text systems, yet have received little attention to date. This position paper maps out a conceptual framework to address this gap, focusing on systems which generate images depicting scenes from scene descriptions. In doing so, we account for how texts and images convey meaning differently. We outline a set of core challenges concerning textual and visual ambiguity, as well as risks that may be amplified by ambiguous and underspecified elements. We propose and discuss strategies for addressing these challenges, including generating visually ambiguous images, and generating a set of diverse images.
abstract_zh: 有关隐含性、歧义性和不明确性的问题对于理解多模态图像+文本系统的任务有效性和伦理问题至关重要，但迄今为止很少受到关注。本立场文件制定了一个概念框架来解决这一差距，重点关注根据场景描述生成描绘场景的图像的系统。在此过程中，我们考虑了文本和图像如何以不同的方式传达含义。我们概述了一系列有关文本和视觉模糊性的核心挑战，以及可能因模糊和不明确的元素而放大的风险。我们提出并讨论了应对这些挑战的策略，包括生成视觉上模糊的图像，以及生成一组不同的图像。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨场景描述到图像生成任务中的未指定性问题，构建概念框架分析挑战与解决方案。
AI.motivation: 现有多模态系统在处理隐含、模糊信息时存在任务有效性与伦理风险，但相关研究不足。
AI.method: 建立理论框架分析文本与图像的语义差异，识别模糊性挑战及风险，并提出生成多样化图像等策略。
AI.result: 提出核心挑战清单及风险放大机制，讨论生成视觉模糊图像与多样化输出等应对方法。
AI.conclusion: 解决未指定性问题是提升多模态系统可靠性与伦理合规性的关键方向。

authors_text: Edward De Brouwer
title: Deep Counterfactual Estimation with Categorical Background Variables
title_zh: 具有分类背景变量的深度反事实估计
abstract: Referred to as the third rung of the causal inference ladder, counterfactual queries typically ask the "What if ?" question retrospectively. The standard approach to estimate counterfactuals resides in using a structural equation model that accurately reflects the underlying data generating process. However, such models are seldom available in practice and one usually wishes to infer them from observational data alone. Unfortunately, the correct structural equation model is in general not identifiable from the observed factual distribution. Nevertheless, in this work, we show that under the assumption that the main latent contributors to the treatment responses are categorical, the counterfactuals can be still reliably predicted. Building upon this assumption, we introduce CounterFactual Query Prediction (CFQP), a novel method to infer counterfactuals from continuous observations when the background variables are categorical. We show that our method significantly outperforms previously available deep-learning-based counterfactual methods, both theoretically and empirically on time series and image data. Our code is available at https://github.com/edebrouwer/cfqp.
summary: Referred to as the third rung of the causal inference ladder, counterfactual queries typically ask the "What if ?" question retrospectively. The standard approach to estimate counterfactuals resides in using a structural equation model that accurately reflects the underlying data generating process. However, such models are seldom available in practice and one usually wishes to infer them from observational data alone. Unfortunately, the correct structural equation model is in general not identifiable from the observed factual distribution. Nevertheless, in this work, we show that under the assumption that the main latent contributors to the treatment responses are categorical, the counterfactuals can be still reliably predicted. Building upon this assumption, we introduce CounterFactual Query Prediction (CFQP), a novel method to infer counterfactuals from continuous observations when the background variables are categorical. We show that our method significantly outperforms previously available deep-learning-based counterfactual methods, both theoretically and empirically on time series and image data. Our code is available at https://github.com/edebrouwer/cfqp.
abstract_zh: 反事实查询被称为因果推理阶梯的第三级，通常会问“如果……会怎样？”回顾性提问。估计反事实的标准方法在于使用准确反映底层数据生成过程的结构方程模型。然而，这样的模型在实践中很少可用，人们通常希望仅根据观测数据来推断它们。不幸的是，正确的结构方程模型通常无法从观察到的事实分布中识别出来。尽管如此，在这项工作中，我们表明，在治疗反应的主要潜在贡献者是分类的假设下，反事实仍然可以可靠地预测。在此假设的基础上，我们引入了 CounterFactual Query Prediction (CFQP)，这是一种当背景变量是分类变量时从连续观察中推断反事实的新方法。我们表明，我们的方法在时间序列和图像数据的理论和经验上都显着优于以前可用的基于深度学习的反事实方法。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/edebrouwer/cfqp 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CFQP的新方法，用于在背景变量为分类变量的情况下，从连续观测数据中可靠地预测反事实。
AI.motivation: 反事实查询通常需要结构方程模型，但在实际中这类模型往往不可用，且从观测数据中识别正确的结构方程模型也存在困难。本文假设主要影响处理响应的潜在变量是分类的，从而提出一种新的方法。
AI.method: 本文引入了CounterFactual Query Prediction (CFQP) 方法，基于分类背景变量的假设，从连续观测数据中推断反事实。
AI.result: 实验表明，CFQP方法在理论和实证上均优于现有的深度学习反事实方法，尤其在时间序列和图像数据上表现突出。
AI.conclusion: 在分类背景变量的假设下，CFQP方法能够可靠地预测反事实，且在多个数据集上表现出优越性。

authors_text: Xi Ye,Guillaume-Alexandre Bilodeau
title: A unified model for continuous conditional video prediction
title_zh: 连续条件视频预测的统一模型
abstract: Different conditional video prediction tasks, like video future frame prediction and video frame interpolation, are normally solved by task-related models even though they share many common underlying characteristics. Furthermore, almost all conditional video prediction models can only achieve discrete prediction. In this paper, we propose a unified model that addresses these two issues at the same time. We show that conditional video prediction can be formulated as a neural process, which maps input spatio-temporal coordinates to target pixel values given context spatio-temporal coordinates and context pixel values. Specifically, we feed the implicit neural representation of coordinates and context pixel features into a Transformer-based non-autoregressive conditional video prediction model. Our task-specific models outperform previous work for video future frame prediction and video interpolation on multiple datasets. Importantly, the model is able to interpolate or predict with an arbitrary high frame rate, i.e., continuous prediction. Our source code is available at \url{https://npvp.github.io}.
summary: Different conditional video prediction tasks, like video future frame prediction and video frame interpolation, are normally solved by task-related models even though they share many common underlying characteristics. Furthermore, almost all conditional video prediction models can only achieve discrete prediction. In this paper, we propose a unified model that addresses these two issues at the same time. We show that conditional video prediction can be formulated as a neural process, which maps input spatio-temporal coordinates to target pixel values given context spatio-temporal coordinates and context pixel values. Specifically, we feed the implicit neural representation of coordinates and context pixel features into a Transformer-based non-autoregressive conditional video prediction model. Our task-specific models outperform previous work for video future frame prediction and video interpolation on multiple datasets. Importantly, the model is able to interpolate or predict with an arbitrary high frame rate, i.e., continuous prediction. Our source code is available at \url{https://npvp.github.io}.
abstract_zh: 不同的条件视频预测任务，例如视频未来帧预测和视频帧插值，通常由任务相关模型来解决，尽管它们具有许多共同的底层特征。此外，几乎所有条件视频预测模型只能实现离散预测。在本文中，我们提出了一个同时解决这两个问题的统一模型。我们证明，条件视频预测可以表示为一个神经过程，它将输入时空坐标映射到给定上下文时空坐标和上下文像素值的目标像素值。具体来说，我们将坐标和上下文像素特征的隐式神经表示输入到基于 Transformer 的非自回归条件视频预测模型中。我们的特定任务模型优于之前在多个数据集上进行视频未来帧预测和视频插值的工作。重要的是，该模型能够以任意高帧速率进行插值或预测，即连续预测。我们的源代码可在 \url{https://npvp.github.io} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种统一的连续条件视频预测模型，能够同时处理视频未来帧预测和帧插值任务。
AI.motivation: 现有条件视频预测模型通常针对特定任务设计，且仅能实现离散预测，无法灵活处理不同任务或高帧率连续预测。
AI.method: 将条件视频预测建模为神经过程，利用坐标和上下文像素特征的隐式表示，结合Transformer非自回归架构实现连续预测。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上优于现有方法，支持任意高帧率的连续预测（插值或未来帧生成）。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过统一框架解决了多任务适配性和连续预测难题，为视频生成领域提供了新思路。

search_text: A unified model for continuous conditional video prediction 连续条件视频预测的统一模型 Different conditional video prediction tasks, like video future frame prediction and video frame interpolation, are normally solved by task-related models even though they share many common underlying characteristics. Furthermore, almost all conditional video prediction models can only achieve discrete prediction. In this paper, we propose a unified model that addresses these two issues at the same time. We show that conditional video prediction can be formulated as a neural process, which maps input spatio-temporal coordinates to target pixel values given context spatio-temporal coordinates and context pixel values. Specifically, we feed the implicit neural representation of coordinates and context pixel features into a Transformer-based non-autoregressive conditional video prediction model. Our task-specific models outperform previous work for video future frame prediction and video interpolation on multiple datasets. Importantly, the model is able to interpolate or predict with an arbitrary high frame rate, i.e., continuous prediction. Our source code is available at \url{https://npvp.github.io}. 不同的条件视频预测任务，例如视频未来帧预测和视频帧插值，通常由任务相关模型来解决，尽管它们具有许多共同的底层特征。此外，几乎所有条件视频预测模型只能实现离散预测。在本文中，我们提出了一个同时解决这两个问题的统一模型。我们证明，条件视频预测可以表示为一个神经过程，它将输入时空坐标映射到给定上下文时空坐标和上下文像素值的目标像素值。具体来说，我们将坐标和上下文像素特征的隐式神经表示输入到基于 Transformer 的非自回归条件视频预测模型中。我们的特定任务模型优于之前在多个数据集上进行视频未来帧预测和视频插值的工作。重要的是，该模型能够以任意高帧速率进行插值或预测，即连续预测。我们的源代码可在 \url{https://npvp.github.io} 获取。 本文提出了一种统一的连续条件视频预测模型，能够同时处理视频未来帧预测和帧插值任务。 Xi Ye,Guillaume-Alexandre Bilodeau cs.CV

authors_text: Mikael Henaff,Roberta Raileanu,Minqi Jiang,Tim Rocktäschel
title: Exploration via Elliptical Episodic Bonuses
title_zh: 通过椭圆情节奖金进行探索
abstract: In recent years, a number of reinforcement learning (RL) methods have been proposed to explore complex environments which differ across episodes. In this work, we show that the effectiveness of these methods critically relies on a count-based episodic term in their exploration bonus. As a result, despite their success in relatively simple, noise-free settings, these methods fall short in more realistic scenarios where the state space is vast and prone to noise. To address this limitation, we introduce Exploration via Elliptical Episodic Bonuses (E3B), a new method which extends count-based episodic bonuses to continuous state spaces and encourages an agent to explore states that are diverse under a learned embedding within each episode. The embedding is learned using an inverse dynamics model in order to capture controllable aspects of the environment. Our method sets a new state-of-the-art across 16 challenging tasks from the MiniHack suite, without requiring task-specific inductive biases. E3B also matches existing methods on sparse reward, pixel-based VizDoom environments, and outperforms existing methods in reward-free exploration on Habitat, demonstrating that it can scale to high-dimensional pixel-based observations and realistic environments.
summary: In recent years, a number of reinforcement learning (RL) methods have been proposed to explore complex environments which differ across episodes. In this work, we show that the effectiveness of these methods critically relies on a count-based episodic term in their exploration bonus. As a result, despite their success in relatively simple, noise-free settings, these methods fall short in more realistic scenarios where the state space is vast and prone to noise. To address this limitation, we introduce Exploration via Elliptical Episodic Bonuses (E3B), a new method which extends count-based episodic bonuses to continuous state spaces and encourages an agent to explore states that are diverse under a learned embedding within each episode. The embedding is learned using an inverse dynamics model in order to capture controllable aspects of the environment. Our method sets a new state-of-the-art across 16 challenging tasks from the MiniHack suite, without requiring task-specific inductive biases. E3B also matches existing methods on sparse reward, pixel-based VizDoom environments, and outperforms existing methods in reward-free exploration on Habitat, demonstrating that it can scale to high-dimensional pixel-based observations and realistic environments.
abstract_zh: 近年来，人们提出了许多强化学习（RL）方法来探索不同场景的复杂环境。在这项工作中，我们表明这些方法的有效性很大程度上依赖于其探索奖励中基于计数的情景术语。因此，尽管这些方法在相对简单、无噪声的设置中取得了成功，但在状态空间巨大且容易产生噪声的更现实的场景中却表现不佳。为了解决这个限制，我们引入了通过椭圆情节奖励（E3B）进行探索，这是一种新方法，它将基于计数的情节奖励扩展到连续的状态空间，并鼓励代理探索每个情节中学习嵌入下的不同状态。嵌入是使用逆动力学模型学习的，以便捕获环境的可控方面。我们的方法在 MiniHack 套件中的 16 个具有挑战性的任务中设定了新的最先进水平，而不需要特定于任务的归纳偏差。 E3B 还与稀疏奖励、基于像素的 VizDoom 环境中的现有方法相匹配，并且在 Habitat 上的无奖励探索中优于现有方法，证明它可以扩展到基于高维像素的观察和现实环境。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为E3B的新方法，通过椭圆式情节奖励机制改进强化学习中的探索策略，以应对复杂环境中的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有基于计数的情节探索方法在简单环境中表现良好，但在大规模、高噪声的现实场景中效果不佳，需改进探索策略以适应连续状态空间。
AI.method: E3B通过逆动力学模型学习环境嵌入，将基于计数的情节奖励扩展到连续状态空间，鼓励智能体在每个情节中探索嵌入空间中多样化的状态。
AI.result: E3B在MiniHack的16个任务中达到最先进水平，在稀疏奖励的VizDoom环境和无奖励探索的Habitat环境中表现优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: E3B通过学习嵌入空间实现高效探索，无需任务特定归纳偏见，适用于高维像素观测和现实环境。

authors_text: Xing Han,Tongzheng Ren,Tan Minh Nguyen,Khai Nguyen,Joydeep Ghosh,Nhat Ho
title: Designing Robust Transformers using Robust Kernel Density Estimation
title_zh: 使用鲁棒核密度估计设计鲁棒变压器
comment: Accepted by NeurIPS 2023 as a poster; 23 pages, 5 figures, 11 tables
comments: Accepted by NeurIPS 2023 as a poster; 23 pages, 5 figures, 11 tables
abstract: Recent advances in Transformer architectures have empowered their empirical success in a variety of tasks across different domains. However, existing works mainly focus on predictive accuracy and computational cost, without considering other practical issues, such as robustness to contaminated samples. Recent work by Nguyen et al., (2022) has shown that the self-attention mechanism, which is the center of the Transformer architecture, can be viewed as a non-parametric estimator based on kernel density estimation (KDE). This motivates us to leverage a set of robust kernel density estimation methods for alleviating the issue of data contamination. Specifically, we introduce a series of self-attention mechanisms that can be incorporated into different Transformer architectures and discuss the special properties of each method. We then perform extensive empirical studies on language modeling and image classification tasks. Our methods demonstrate robust performance in multiple scenarios while maintaining competitive results on clean datasets.
summary: Recent advances in Transformer architectures have empowered their empirical success in a variety of tasks across different domains. However, existing works mainly focus on predictive accuracy and computational cost, without considering other practical issues, such as robustness to contaminated samples. Recent work by Nguyen et al., (2022) has shown that the self-attention mechanism, which is the center of the Transformer architecture, can be viewed as a non-parametric estimator based on kernel density estimation (KDE). This motivates us to leverage a set of robust kernel density estimation methods for alleviating the issue of data contamination. Specifically, we introduce a series of self-attention mechanisms that can be incorporated into different Transformer architectures and discuss the special properties of each method. We then perform extensive empirical studies on language modeling and image classification tasks. Our methods demonstrate robust performance in multiple scenarios while maintaining competitive results on clean datasets.
abstract_zh: Transformer 架构的最新进展使他们能够在不同领域的各种任务中取得经验上的成功。然而，现有的工作主要关注预测准确性和计算成本，而没有考虑其他实际问题，例如对污染样本的鲁棒性。 Nguyen 等人 (2022) 最近的工作表明，作为 Transformer 架构中心的自注意力机制可以被视为基于核密度估计 (KDE) 的非参数估计器。这促使我们利用一套强大的核密度估计方法来减轻数据污染问题。具体来说，我们介绍了一系列可以合并到不同 Transformer 架构中的自注意力机制，并讨论了每种方法的特殊属性。然后，我们对语言建模和图像分类任务进行广泛的实证研究。我们的方法在多种场景中展示了强大的性能，同时在干净的数据集上保持了有竞争力的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在设计具有鲁棒性的Transformer架构，以解决数据污染问题。
AI.motivation: 现有Transformer研究侧重于准确率和计算成本，忽视了对污染样本的鲁棒性。Nguyen等人将自注意力机制与核密度估计（KDE）关联，启发了作者引入鲁棒KDE方法。
AI.method: 提出基于鲁棒KDE的自注意力机制，改进Transformer架构，并分析其特性。
AI.result: 在语言建模和图像分类任务中，方法在污染数据下表现鲁棒，且在干净数据集上保持竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 鲁棒KDE方法有效提升Transformer对数据污染的抵御能力，同时不牺牲清洁数据下的性能。

conclusion: 鲁棒KDE方法有效提升Transformer对数据污染的抵御能力，同时不牺牲清洁数据下的性能。
search_text: Designing Robust Transformers using Robust Kernel Density Estimation 使用鲁棒核密度估计设计鲁棒变压器 Recent advances in Transformer architectures have empowered their empirical success in a variety of tasks across different domains. However, existing works mainly focus on predictive accuracy and computational cost, without considering other practical issues, such as robustness to contaminated samples. Recent work by Nguyen et al., (2022) has shown that the self-attention mechanism, which is the center of the Transformer architecture, can be viewed as a non-parametric estimator based on kernel density estimation (KDE). This motivates us to leverage a set of robust kernel density estimation methods for alleviating the issue of data contamination. Specifically, we introduce a series of self-attention mechanisms that can be incorporated into different Transformer architectures and discuss the special properties of each method. We then perform extensive empirical studies on language modeling and image classification tasks. Our methods demonstrate robust performance in multiple scenarios while maintaining competitive results on clean datasets. Transformer 架构的最新进展使他们能够在不同领域的各种任务中取得经验上的成功。然而，现有的工作主要关注预测准确性和计算成本，而没有考虑其他实际问题，例如对污染样本的鲁棒性。 Nguyen 等人 (2022) 最近的工作表明，作为 Transformer 架构中心的自注意力机制可以被视为基于核密度估计 (KDE) 的非参数估计器。这促使我们利用一套强大的核密度估计方法来减轻数据污染问题。具体来说，我们介绍了一系列可以合并到不同 Transformer 架构中的自注意力机制，并讨论了每种方法的特殊属性。然后，我们对语言建模和图像分类任务进行广泛的实证研究。我们的方法在多种场景中展示了强大的性能，同时在干净的数据集上保持了有竞争力的结果。 本文旨在设计具有鲁棒性的Transformer架构，以解决数据污染问题。 Xing Han,Tongzheng Ren,Tan Minh Nguyen,Khai Nguyen,Joydeep Ghosh,Nhat Ho cs.LG,cs.CL,cs.CV

authors_text: Dongseong Hwang,Khe Chai Sim,Yu Zhang,Trevor Strohman
title: Comparison of Soft and Hard Target RNN-T Distillation for Large-scale ASR
title_zh: 大规模 ASR 软目标 RNN-T 蒸馏与硬目标蒸馏的比较
abstract: Knowledge distillation is an effective machine learning technique to transfer knowledge from a teacher model to a smaller student model, especially with unlabeled data. In this paper, we focus on knowledge distillation for the RNN-T model, which is widely used in state-of-the-art (SoTA) automatic speech recognition (ASR). Specifically, we compared using soft and hard target distillation to train large-scaleRNN-T models on the LibriSpeech/LibriLight public dataset (60k hours) and our in-house data (600k hours). We found that hard tar-gets are more effective when the teacher and student have different architecture, such as large teacher and small streaming student. On the other hand, soft target distillation works better in self-training scenario like iterative large teacher training. For a large model with0.6B weights, we achieve a new SoTA word error rate (WER) on LibriSpeech (8% relative improvement on dev-other) using Noisy Student Training with soft target distillation. It also allows our production teacher to adapt new data domain continuously.
summary: Knowledge distillation is an effective machine learning technique to transfer knowledge from a teacher model to a smaller student model, especially with unlabeled data. In this paper, we focus on knowledge distillation for the RNN-T model, which is widely used in state-of-the-art (SoTA) automatic speech recognition (ASR). Specifically, we compared using soft and hard target distillation to train large-scaleRNN-T models on the LibriSpeech/LibriLight public dataset (60k hours) and our in-house data (600k hours). We found that hard tar-gets are more effective when the teacher and student have different architecture, such as large teacher and small streaming student. On the other hand, soft target distillation works better in self-training scenario like iterative large teacher training. For a large model with0.6B weights, we achieve a new SoTA word error rate (WER) on LibriSpeech (8% relative improvement on dev-other) using Noisy Student Training with soft target distillation. It also allows our production teacher to adapt new data domain continuously.
abstract_zh: 知识蒸馏是一种有效的机器学习技术，可将知识从教师模型转移到较小的学生模型，尤其是对于未标记的数据。在本文中，我们重点关注 RNN-T 模型的知识蒸馏，该模型广泛应用于最先进的 (SoTA) 自动语音识别 (ASR)。具体来说，我们比较了使用软目标蒸馏和硬目标蒸馏在 LibriSpeech/LibriLight 公共数据集（6 万小时）和我们的内部数据（60 万小时）上训练大规模 RNN-T 模型。我们发现，当教师和学生具有不同的架构时，例如大型教师和小型流式学生，硬目标会更有效。另一方面，软目标蒸馏在迭代大型教师培训等自我训练场景中效果更好。对于权重为 0.6B 的大型模型，我们使用带有软目标蒸馏的 Noisy Student Training 在 LibriSpeech 上实现了新的 SoTA 单词错误率 (WER)（相对于其他开发者提高了 8%）。它还允许我们的生产老师不断适应新的数据领域。

AI.tldr: 比较软目标和硬目标RNN-T知识蒸馏在大规模语音识别中的效果
AI.motivation: 知识蒸馏能有效利用未标记数据提升模型性能，但不同架构下蒸馏效果差异未明确，需探索最优方法
AI.method: 在LibriSpeech/LibriLight及内部600k小时数据上对比软硬目标蒸馏，分析架构差异与自训练场景下的效果
AI.result: 硬目标在异构架构（大教师-小学生）中更优，软目标在自训练场景表现更好，0.6B参数模型在LibriSpeech上实现8%相对WER提升
AI.conclusion: 应根据教师学生架构差异及训练场景选择蒸馏方式，软目标蒸馏结合Noisy Student训练可提升模型泛化能力

authors_text: Renee Shelby,Shalaleh Rismani,Kathryn Henne,AJung Moon,Negar Rostamzadeh,Paul Nicholas,N'Mah Yilla,Jess Gallegos,Andrew Smart,Emilio Garcia,Gurleen Virk
title: Sociotechnical Harms of Algorithmic Systems: Scoping a Taxonomy for Harm Reduction
title_zh: 算法系统的社会技术危害：确定减少危害的分类法范围
abstract: Understanding the landscape of potential harms from algorithmic systems enables practitioners to better anticipate consequences of the systems they build. It also supports the prospect of incorporating controls to help minimize harms that emerge from the interplay of technologies and social and cultural dynamics. A growing body of scholarship has identified a wide range of harms across different algorithmic technologies. However, computing research and practitioners lack a high level and synthesized overview of harms from algorithmic systems. Based on a scoping review of computing research $(n=172)$, we present an applied taxonomy of sociotechnical harms to support a more systematic surfacing of potential harms in algorithmic systems. The final taxonomy builds on and refers to existing taxonomies, classifications, and terminologies. Five major themes related to sociotechnical harms - representational, allocative, quality-of-service, interpersonal harms, and social system/societal harms - and sub-themes are presented along with a description of these categories. We conclude with a discussion of challenges and opportunities for future research.
summary: Understanding the landscape of potential harms from algorithmic systems enables practitioners to better anticipate consequences of the systems they build. It also supports the prospect of incorporating controls to help minimize harms that emerge from the interplay of technologies and social and cultural dynamics. A growing body of scholarship has identified a wide range of harms across different algorithmic technologies. However, computing research and practitioners lack a high level and synthesized overview of harms from algorithmic systems. Based on a scoping review of computing research $(n=172)$, we present an applied taxonomy of sociotechnical harms to support a more systematic surfacing of potential harms in algorithmic systems. The final taxonomy builds on and refers to existing taxonomies, classifications, and terminologies. Five major themes related to sociotechnical harms - representational, allocative, quality-of-service, interpersonal harms, and social system/societal harms - and sub-themes are presented along with a description of these categories. We conclude with a discussion of challenges and opportunities for future research.
abstract_zh: 了解算法系统潜在危害的情况使从业者能够更好地预测他们构建的系统的后果。它还支持纳入控制措施的前景，以帮助最大限度地减少技术与社会和文化动态相互作用所产生的危害。越来越多的学术机构已经发现不同算法技术存在广泛的危害。然而，计算研究和从业者缺乏对算法系统危害的高层次综合概述。基于对计算研究 $(n=172)$ 的范围审查，我们提出了社会技术危害的应用分类法，以支持更系统地呈现算法系统中的潜在危害。最终的分类法建立在现有的分类法、分类法和术语的基础上并引用了现有的分类法、分类法和术语。与社会技术危害相关的五个主要主题——代表性、分配性、服务质量、人际危害和社会系统/社会危害——以及子主题以及这些类别的描述。最后我们讨论了未来研究的挑战和机遇。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在构建一个系统化的社会技术危害分类体系，以帮助识别和减少算法系统带来的潜在危害。
AI.motivation: 现有研究虽已识别多种算法危害，但缺乏对危害的高层级综合概述，阻碍了从业者有效应对技术与社会动态交互带来的问题。
AI.method: 通过范围综述方法分析172篇计算领域研究，整合现有分类框架，提炼出五类核心社会技术危害主题。
AI.result: 提出包含代表性和分配性危害、服务质量危害、人际危害及社会系统危害等五大主题的分类体系，并描述其子类与特征。
AI.conclusion: 该分类体系为危害识别提供系统工具，但需进一步验证其适用性，并探索如何将分类结果转化为实际危害缓解策略。

authors_text: Guilherme Lourenço de Toledo,Ricardo Marcondes Marcacini
title: Transfer Learning with Joint Fine-Tuning for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
title_zh: 用于多模态情感分析的联合微调的迁移学习
abstract: Most existing methods focus on sentiment analysis of textual data. However, recently there has been a massive use of images and videos on social platforms, motivating sentiment analysis from other modalities. Current studies show that exploring other modalities (e.g., images) increases sentiment analysis performance. State-of-the-art multimodal models, such as CLIP and VisualBERT, are pre-trained on datasets with the text paired with images. Although the results obtained by these models are promising, pre-training and sentiment analysis fine-tuning tasks of these models are computationally expensive. This paper introduces a transfer learning approach using joint fine-tuning for sentiment analysis. Our proposal achieved competitive results using a more straightforward alternative fine-tuning strategy that leverages different pre-trained unimodal models and efficiently combines them in a multimodal space. Moreover, our proposal allows flexibility when incorporating any pre-trained model for texts and images during the joint fine-tuning stage, being especially interesting for sentiment classification in low-resource scenarios.
summary: Most existing methods focus on sentiment analysis of textual data. However, recently there has been a massive use of images and videos on social platforms, motivating sentiment analysis from other modalities. Current studies show that exploring other modalities (e.g., images) increases sentiment analysis performance. State-of-the-art multimodal models, such as CLIP and VisualBERT, are pre-trained on datasets with the text paired with images. Although the results obtained by these models are promising, pre-training and sentiment analysis fine-tuning tasks of these models are computationally expensive. This paper introduces a transfer learning approach using joint fine-tuning for sentiment analysis. Our proposal achieved competitive results using a more straightforward alternative fine-tuning strategy that leverages different pre-trained unimodal models and efficiently combines them in a multimodal space. Moreover, our proposal allows flexibility when incorporating any pre-trained model for texts and images during the joint fine-tuning stage, being especially interesting for sentiment classification in low-resource scenarios.
abstract_zh: 大多数现有方法侧重于文本数据的情感分析。然而，最近社交平台上大量使用图像和视频，激发了其他方式的情感分析。目前的研究表明，探索其他模式（例如图像）可以提高情感分析性能。最先进的多模态模型，例如 CLIP 和 VisualBERT，是在文本与图像配对的数据集上进行预训练的。尽管这些模型获得的结果很有希望，但这些模型的预训练和情感分析微调任务的计算成本很高。本文介绍了一种使用联合微调进行情感分析的迁移学习方法。我们的建议使用更直接的替代微调策略取得了有竞争力的结果，该策略利用不同的预训练单峰模型并将它们有效地组合在多峰空间中。此外，我们的建议允许在联合微调阶段结合任何预先训练的文本和图像模型时具有灵活性，这对于低资源场景中的情感分类特别有趣。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于联合微调的迁移学习方法，用于多模态情感分析，结合文本和图像模态以提高性能。
AI.motivation: 现有模型预训练和微调成本高，且难以灵活结合不同预训练模型，尤其在低资源场景下需要更高效的方法。
AI.method: 采用联合微调策略，结合文本和图像的预训练模型，在多模态空间中进行有效融合，提升情感分析性能。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在多个数据集上达到竞争性性能，尤其在低资源场景下表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 该方法提供了一种高效灵活的多模态情感分析框架，适用于低资源场景，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Transfer Learning with Joint Fine-Tuning for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis 用于多模态情感分析的联合微调的迁移学习 Most existing methods focus on sentiment analysis of textual data. However, recently there has been a massive use of images and videos on social platforms, motivating sentiment analysis from other modalities. Current studies show that exploring other modalities (e.g., images) increases sentiment analysis performance. State-of-the-art multimodal models, such as CLIP and VisualBERT, are pre-trained on datasets with the text paired with images. Although the results obtained by these models are promising, pre-training and sentiment analysis fine-tuning tasks of these models are computationally expensive. This paper introduces a transfer learning approach using joint fine-tuning for sentiment analysis. Our proposal achieved competitive results using a more straightforward alternative fine-tuning strategy that leverages different pre-trained unimodal models and efficiently combines them in a multimodal space. Moreover, our proposal allows flexibility when incorporating any pre-trained model for texts and images during the joint fine-tuning stage, being especially interesting for sentiment classification in low-resource scenarios. 大多数现有方法侧重于文本数据的情感分析。然而，最近社交平台上大量使用图像和视频，激发了其他方式的情感分析。目前的研究表明，探索其他模式（例如图像）可以提高情感分析性能。最先进的多模态模型，例如 CLIP 和 VisualBERT，是在文本与图像配对的数据集上进行预训练的。尽管这些模型获得的结果很有希望，但这些模型的预训练和情感分析微调任务的计算成本很高。本文介绍了一种使用联合微调进行情感分析的迁移学习方法。我们的建议使用更直接的替代微调策略取得了有竞争力的结果，该策略利用不同的预训练单峰模型并将它们有效地组合在多峰空间中。此外，我们的建议允许在联合微调阶段结合任何预先训练的文本和图像模型时具有灵活性，这对于低资源场景中的情感分类特别有趣。 提出一种基于联合微调的迁移学习方法，用于多模态情感分析，结合文本和图像模态以提高性能。 Guilherme Lourenço de Toledo,Ricardo Marcondes Marcacini cs.LG,cs.CL,cs.CV

authors_text: Ke Hu,Bo Li,Tara N. Sainath
title: Scaling Up Deliberation for Multilingual ASR
title_zh: 扩大多语言 ASR 的审议范围
abstract: Multilingual end-to-end automatic speech recognition models are attractive due to its simplicity in training and deployment. Recent work on large-scale training of such models has shown promising results compared to monolingual models. However, the work often focuses on multilingual models themselves in a single-pass setup. In this work, we investigate second-pass deliberation for multilingual speech recognition. Our proposed deliberation is multilingual, i.e., the text encoder encodes hypothesis text from multiple languages, and the decoder attends to multilingual text and audio. We investigate scaling the deliberation text encoder and decoder, and compare scaling the deliberation decoder and the first-pass cascaded encoder. We show that deliberation improves the average WER on 9 languages by 4% relative compared to the single-pass model. By increasing the size of the deliberation up to 1B parameters, the average WER improvement increases to 9%, with up to 14% for certain languages. Our deliberation rescorer is based on transformer layers and can be parallelized during rescoring.
summary: Multilingual end-to-end automatic speech recognition models are attractive due to its simplicity in training and deployment. Recent work on large-scale training of such models has shown promising results compared to monolingual models. However, the work often focuses on multilingual models themselves in a single-pass setup. In this work, we investigate second-pass deliberation for multilingual speech recognition. Our proposed deliberation is multilingual, i.e., the text encoder encodes hypothesis text from multiple languages, and the decoder attends to multilingual text and audio. We investigate scaling the deliberation text encoder and decoder, and compare scaling the deliberation decoder and the first-pass cascaded encoder. We show that deliberation improves the average WER on 9 languages by 4% relative compared to the single-pass model. By increasing the size of the deliberation up to 1B parameters, the average WER improvement increases to 9%, with up to 14% for certain languages. Our deliberation rescorer is based on transformer layers and can be parallelized during rescoring.
abstract_zh: 多语言端到端自动语音识别模型因其训练和部署的简单性而颇具吸引力。与单语言模型相比，最近对此类模型进行大规模训练的工作显示出有希望的结果。然而，这项工作通常侧重于单遍设置中的多语言模型本身。在这项工作中，我们研究了多语言语音识别的第二遍审议。我们提出的审议是多语言的，即文本编码器对来自多种语言的假设文本进行编码，而解码器则处理多语言文本和音频。我们研究了协商文本编码器和解码器的缩放，并比较了协商解码器和第一遍级联编码器的缩放。我们发现，与单遍模型相比，深思熟虑将 9 种语言的平均 WER 提高了 4%。通过将审议规模增加到 1B 个参数，平均 WER 改进增加到 9%，对于某些语言高达 14%。我们的审议重新评分器基于变压器层，并且可以在重新评分期间并行化。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了多语言语音识别中第二轮推理（deliberation）方法的扩展，以提高识别准确性。
AI.motivation: 虽然多语言端到端语音识别模型在训练和部署上具有优势，但现有研究通常仅关注单次推理设置，而未充分探索多语言第二轮推理的潜力。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种多语言的第二轮推理方法，其中文本编码器对多种语言的假设文本进行编码，解码器则同时关注多语言文本和音频。通过扩展推理的文本编码器和解码器，并对比扩展推理解码器和第一轮级联编码器的效果，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明，与单次推理模型相比，第二轮推理在9种语言上的平均词错误率（WER）提高了4%。当推理模型扩展到10亿参数时，平均WER提升达到9%，某些语言甚至达到14%。
AI.conclusion: 多语言第二轮推理方法能有效提升多语言语音识别的性能，且基于Transformer的推理重评分器可并行处理，具有良好的扩展性。

authors_text: Yusuke Tanaka,Ankur Mehta
title: REMS: Middleware for Robotics Education and Development
title_zh: REMS：机器人教育和开发中间件
abstract: This paper introduces REMS, a robotics middleware and control framework that is designed to introduce the Zen of Python to robotics and to improve robotics education and development flow. Although existing middleware can serve hardware abstraction and modularity, setting up environments and learning middleware-specific syntax and procedures are less viable in education. They can curb opportunities to understand robotics concepts, theories, and algorithms. Robotics is a field of integration; students and developers from various backgrounds will be involved in programming. Establishing Pythonic and object-oriented robotic framework in a natural way can enhance modular and abstracted programming for better readability, reusability, and simplicity, but also supports useful and practical skills generally in coding. REMS is to be a valuable robot educational medium not just as a tool and to be a platform from one robot to multi-agent across hardware, simulation, and analytical model implementations.
summary: This paper introduces REMS, a robotics middleware and control framework that is designed to introduce the Zen of Python to robotics and to improve robotics education and development flow. Although existing middleware can serve hardware abstraction and modularity, setting up environments and learning middleware-specific syntax and procedures are less viable in education. They can curb opportunities to understand robotics concepts, theories, and algorithms. Robotics is a field of integration; students and developers from various backgrounds will be involved in programming. Establishing Pythonic and object-oriented robotic framework in a natural way can enhance modular and abstracted programming for better readability, reusability, and simplicity, but also supports useful and practical skills generally in coding. REMS is to be a valuable robot educational medium not just as a tool and to be a platform from one robot to multi-agent across hardware, simulation, and analytical model implementations.
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 REMS，这是一种机器人中间件和控制框架，旨在将 Python 之禅引入机器人并改善机器人教育和开发流程。尽管现有的中间件可以服务于硬件抽象和模块化，但设置环境和学习中间件特定的语法和过程在教育中不太可行。它们可以限制理解机器人概念、理论和算法的机会。机器人技术是一个集成领域；来自不同背景的学生和开发人员将参与编程。以自然的方式建立Pythonic和面向对象的机器人框架可以增强模块化和抽象编程，以获得更好的可读性、可重用性和简单性，而且还支持编码中有用和实用的技能。 REMS 将成为一种有价值的机器人教育媒介，而不仅仅是一种工具，而且是一个跨硬件、模拟和分析模型实现的从一个机器人到多智能体的平台。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍REMS中间件，旨在通过Python哲学优化机器人教育和开发流程，提升模块化编程与跨平台支持。
AI.motivation: 现有机器人中间件在教育中存在环境配置复杂、语法学习门槛高，限制学生对核心概念的理解，需更自然的框架支持多样化背景开发者。
AI.method: 构建基于Pythonic理念的面向对象框架，实现硬件抽象、模块化编程及跨硬件/模拟/分析模型的多代理系统支持。
AI.result: REMS作为教育工具和跨平台开发媒介，提升代码可读性、复用性及开发效率，促进机器人教育实践。
AI.conclusion: REMS通过Python化设计有效改善机器人教育与开发流程，为多场景应用提供模块化解决方案。
